themes.exampleQuote: I always make sure to try their new flavors—it always surprises me.
That gooey butter cake ice cream has me in a chokehold.
Super expensive... and the portion of these two standards is mind boggling.
I loved the waffle cones which they make fresh throughout the day.
Me and my dog's favorite spot—I always order him a small scoop of vanilla with waffle wedge.

notable_quote: That gooey butter cake ice cream has me in a chokehold.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently come back for the rotating seasonal flavors and the freshness of both the ice cream and the waffle cones, while the most persistent friction is a feeling that portion sizes don't justify the price at this specific Pittsburgh location.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-nine Pittsburgh creators filmed Jeni's this quarter. Jeni's filmed none of it back. The business has no TikTok presence of its own, yet 30-plus videos tagged to the Larimer location have stacked up 147,000 plays, with @justapittsburghgirl's gooey butter cake reel alone pulling 48,400—a number Jeni's own Instagram hasn't come close to touching this month. That Instagram, posting 12 times in 30 days at a 0.49 percent engagement rate, is active but largely invisible against the creator noise already being made on its behalf. In the Pittsburgh Sweets family, Page's holds the lead this issue on social momentum; Jeni's holds the stronger creator ecosystem and the better-documented visual catalog, but those advantages aren't fully expressing themselves in the rankings yet. If that TikTok groundswell ever connects to Jeni's own channels—or if engagement on existing posts begins to reflect the actual foot traffic the creator videos are driving—the gap between #3 and first narrows fast.
tldr_read: 147,000 TikTok plays from other people's accounts, no TikTok of its own, and a Google conversion path that is flawless—Icons of the Burgh, #2 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The creator signal is already doing heavy lifting; the business's own channels are not keeping pace, and that asymmetry is the single variable holding this ranking where it is rather than higher.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok ground already yours
playbook.action: 29 creators have done the work—launching even a minimal account to stitch or repost the 48K-play gooey butter cake video stops ceding that traffic entirely.

playbook.headline: Convert Instagram activity into actual engagement
playbook.action: 12 posts in 30 days is consistent, but 0.49% engagement suggests the content isn't landing—tighter creative focus on the flavor-specific moments driving TikTok views would close that gap.

playbook.headline: Address the portion-value conversation directly
playbook.action: At least two reviews independently flag the price-to-portion ratio at this location; acknowledging or adjusting it removes the only recurring friction pulling Review Sentiment below the family median.
